title: Wet felted wall art panel
subtitle: A layered landscape panel in three to five merino colours mounted on a wooden dowel
excerpt: Wet felt a 60 x 40 cm landscape panel using horizon-band colour layering in three to five merino colours. The finished panel is hung from a wooden dowel threaded through a casing at the top. The technique suits abstract landscapes: graduated sky, a middle field, and a foreground in contrasting tones.
sourceNotes: Flat felt landscape panels for wall display are a popular contemporary textile art form. Method draws on layering and blending techniques common in contemporary felt-art workshops.

glossaryTerms.definition: A colour-layout technique in flat felt where roving strips are laid in horizontal bands across the piece, each band a different colour. The colour shift across the piece reads as a landscape or abstract gradient when viewed from a distance.

content.text: A landscape panel works because wet felt naturally creates a softly textured surface that reads as terrain, sky, and water from a distance.
controls the colour placement. The bands are blended at their edges to avoid hard stripes; any variation in the blending makes the landscape read more naturally.

items.name: Bamboo rolling mat (at least 75 x 50 cm)
Pool noodle or foam tube for rolling

content.text: Before laying roving, arrange the colour bands in order from sky at the top to foreground at the bottom. Five-band suggestion: pale grey-blue (sky), cream or white (mist or cloud zone), soft green (distant hills), deeper green (mid-field), warm ochre or dark earth (foreground). Lay the merino bands in this order from top to bottom on the mat.

Lay each colour band in two perpendicular layers (horizontal first, then vertical) to a depth of about 3-4 mm of roving. Blend adjacent bands at their boundaries by teasing the fibres together. Extend the layout about 6 cm beyond the planned final dimensions to allow for shrinkage.

Wet out through the felting net, rub for 15 minutes, flip and rub the reverse side. Roll around the pool noodle, 50 passes, turn 90 degrees, 50 more passes. Repeat until the panel has shrunk to approximately the target dimensions and the felt is firm throughout.
Step 3: Add hanging casing and finish

While the panel is still damp, fold 4 cm of the top edge down toward the back of the panel and press firmly with your palm to begin felting the fold in place. Return the fold to the mat and rub it against itself with soapy hands until it felts together into a tube. Thread the wooden dowel through the tube when dry. Tie hanging cord to each end of the dowel.

items.problem: The panel curls or bows when hung
items.cause: Uneven fulling or hung before fully dry
items.fix: Block the wet panel flat by pinning it to a foam board with the dowel in place and allow to dry fully before hanging.

items.problem: The colour bands look striped rather than landscape-like
items.cause: Boundaries not blended thoroughly before felting
items.fix: At the boundary zone, thin each colour down to a few fibres and interleave them by hand before wetting. The boundary should be invisible before the water goes on.
